programme.title: FNF: Leeds v West Ham Hlts
programme.desc: Looking to navigate a tricky spell in the early throws of his West Ham tenure, Nuno Espirito Santo takes his side to Elland Road for a Friday Night Football meeting with Leeds. (24.10)

programme.title: PL: Chelsea v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: Separated by goal difference, the Black Cats arrive at the Bridge level on 14 points with the hosts as Regis Le Bris' impressive Sunderland outfit challenge Chelsea on the road. (25.10)

programme.title: PL: Newcastle v Fulham Hlts
programme.desc: On song at St James' Park midweek, can an up-and-down Newcastle replicate their European form domestically as Fulham come next for Eddie Howe and co in the Premier League? (25.10)

programme.title: SNF: Man Utd v Brighton Hlts
programme.desc: On Saturday Night Football, Manchester United host Brighton after a memorable win at Anfield. Ex-United man Danny Welbeck scored twice in his side's win over Newcastle previously. (25.10)

programme.title: PL: Wolves v Burnley Hlts
programme.desc: It's a high-stakes afternoon for Vítor Pereira at Molineux against Scott Parker's Burnley as Wolves target an elusive Premier League victory at the ninth time of asking this season. (26.10)

programme.title: PL: Bournemouth v N. Forest Hlts
programme.desc: With the Sean Dyche era up and running at Forest, his new side face one of the Premier League's toughest tests on Super Sunday - a clash with Andoni Iraola's Bournemouth. (26.10)

programme.title: PL: Aston Villa v Man City Hlts
programme.desc: Erling Haaland and Manchester City are the visitors for this Super Sunday clash at Villa. The frightening Haaland notched his 24th goal of the season for club and country midweek. (26.10)

programme.title: PL: Arsenal v Crystal Palace Hlts
programme.desc: After a statement win in Europe midweek, Premier league leaders Arsenal welcome Crystal Palace to the Emirates on Super Sunday. The Eagles played an electric 3-3 draw last Saturday. (26.10)

programme.title: PL: Everton v Spurs Hlts
programme.desc: A chance for Everton and Spurs to return to winning ways in the Premier League - with both teams tasting defeat last week - as Super Sunday comes from the Hill Dickinson Stadium. (26.10)

programme.title: PL: Nott'm Forest v Man Utd Hlts
programme.desc: Sean Dyche takes charge of his first Premier League home game at Nottingham Forest with Manchester United the visitors. Ruben Amorim's men target a fourth consecutive league win. (01.11)

programme.title: PL: Burnley v Arsenal Hlts
programme.desc: An in-form Burnley come next for the Premier League leaders Arsenal. Claiming four wins on the spin, the Gunners have built a four-point cushion as their title bid gathers momentum. (01.11)

programme.title: PL: C. Palace v Brentford Hlts
programme.desc: Crystal Palace attempt to iron out a dip in form as Oliver Glasner's side - who have picked up just a point in their last three - take on a buoyant Brentford at Selhurst Park. (01.11)

programme.title: PL: Brighton v Leeds Hlts
programme.desc: In a Premier League clash at the Amex Stadium, Brighton entertain Daniel Farke's Leeds. The visitors, sat in 15th, can leapfrog the hosts with three points here on the South Coast. (01.11)

programme.title: PL: Fulham v Wolves Hlts
programme.desc: Fulham host struggling Wolves in the Premier League. Rooted to the foot of the table, it was heartbreak last weekend for Wolves - losing in the dying embers to Burnley. (01.11)

programme.title: SNF: Spurs v Chelsea Hlts
programme.desc: Saturday Night Football comes from the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium as Spurs host rivals Chelsea. It's the hosts who are the form team, coming from a 3-0 Premier League win at Everton. (01.11)

programme.title: PL: West Ham v Newcastle Hlts
programme.desc: On Super Sunday, Newcastle visit the capital for a Premier League contest with Nuno Espirito Santo's struggling West Ham - still searching for their first win under the Portuguese. (02.11)

programme.title: PL: Man City v Bournemouth Hlts
programme.desc: Stifled at Villa Park, Pep Guardiola's Manchester City face another stern test this Super Sunday with the Premier League visit of Andoni Iraola's Bournemouth to the Etihad Stadium. (02.11)

programme.title: MNF: Sunderland v Everton Hlts
programme.desc: Regis Le Bris' Black Cats face Everton and ex-manager David Moyes. It was under Moyes during the 16-17 campaign Sunderland suffered relegation, only now returning to the top fight. (03.11)
